criteria_map: Config paths and values identified: satisfied.
Prod promotion automatic/manual policy identified: satisfied, prod is API promotion-only.
Dry-run/guardrails identified: satisfied, `dry_run` only when explicit; polling only diagnostic dry-run; deploy scripts guarded by controller env.
evidence: `docs/architecture/release-controller.md` documents local API `127.0.0.1:19880`, polling disabled, explicit `/v1/triggers`, and prod promotion-only policy.
`docs/runbooks/deploy.md` says Release Controller is the only backend/Factory release entry and direct backend image deploy entries fail outside controller identity.
`config.sample.json` defines `main -> staging`, health URLs, registry image names, quality gates, and verify commands.
`service.py` exposes `/v1/triggers`, `/v1/promotions/prod`, `/v1/runs`, `/v1/status`; `planner.py` rejects branch releases to prod and plans deploy steps.
execution_map: T002 executed read-only local contract discovery.
R000 records the local contract and gaps delegated to P006.
residual_risk: Live host config may differ from sample config; P006 will probe the actual controller.
stress_test: Cross-checked docs against code and sample config so this is not just runbook prose.
C000.summary: P005 is solved by R000. Local Release Controller contract is clear enough to proceed to live probing and later trigger: `main` branch releases to `staging` through `/v1/triggers`, prod is promotion-only, and direct deploy scripts are controller internals.

criteria_map: Reachable base URL discovered: satisfied, public read-only `/cicd` and host-local write base via SSH.
Status/runs captured: satisfied, current staging/prod pointers and recent runs recorded.
Current latest run/pointers known before trigger: satisfied.
evidence: API-host local `/health` returned healthy through SSH.
Live config confirms branch rules, quality gates, verify commands, health URLs, and polling disabled.
Docker shows the release-controller container is healthy and bound to `127.0.0.1:19880`.
execution_map: T003 executed live read-only probing and host-local health/config checks.
No release-triggering side effects were performed.
residual_risk: A future trigger can still fail in build/deploy stages; P006 only proves the controller is currently reachable and configured.
stress_test: Checked both public ingress and host-local API path to distinguish dashboard access from write/control access.
C001.summary: P006 is solved by R001. The live controller is reachable, healthy, and has known current release pointers. Later deployment can use host-local `POST /v1/triggers` via SSH.

criteria_map: Controller config/API and branch policy known: satisfied.
Controller reachable and current status/runs reported: satisfied.
Direct deploy scripts confirmed as controller internals: satisfied by docs and planner/deploy contract.
Exact trigger commands/URLs available: satisfied, use host-local `/v1/triggers` via SSH for `main` + pushed commit.
evidence: P005/C000 identifies API, branch rules, prod promotion policy, deploy steps, quality gates, and health URLs from docs/source/config.
P006/C001 proves live controller health, public read-only status/rules/runs, host-local write base, and current staging/prod pointers.
execution_map: T001 split P001 into local contract inspection and live probing.
Both children closed with success checks before R002.
residual_risk: Build/deploy may still fail after code push; that is handled by later deployment phases.
stress_test: Verified both code/docs and live controller behavior rather than trusting one source.
C002.summary: P001 is solved by R002. The controller contract and live status are known, including exact trigger path and current release baseline.

criteria_map: Git status captured before commit: satisfied.
evidence: Git status snapshots identify intended modified/untracked files and unrelated root `CLAUDE.md`.
execution_map: T005 ran fixed test commands and status capture.
R003 records outputs and known staging caveat.
residual_risk: Release Controller will still run its own quality gates after push.
stress_test: Verification covers server-relevant repos and App source changes before pushing.
C003.summary: P007 is solved by R003. Fresh focused tests passed in all touched repos and dirty files are known for explicit staging.

criteria_map: Factory commit exists on origin/main: satisfied.
Runtime commit exists on origin/main: satisfied.
App commit exists on origin/main: satisfied.
Commit SHAs and push results recorded: satisfied.
evidence: Post-push `git status --short --branch` in each touched subrepo shows `main...origin/main` and no dirty files.
execution_map: T006 staged explicit files in each subrepo, committed, pushed, and captured SHAs.
residual_risk: Root must still commit the submodule pointers before Release Controller can deploy these SHAs.
stress_test: Verified clean status after push, not just command success output.
C004.summary: P008 is solved by R004. All touched subrepos are committed, pushed, and clean.

P000.description: The reasoning streaming changes are implemented locally across `novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`, and `novaic-app`, but they are not yet published. The user wants the complete CI/CD deployment flow, using Release Controller rather than manual deployment scripts, until publication succeeds; at minimum the server-side release must be deployed and health-verified.
success_criteria: Construction changes are committed in all touched subrepos and the root repo records the updated submodule pointers and ledger state.
Code is pushed to the canonical remote branch used by the Release Controller.
Release Controller is triggered through its supported API/control plane, not by direct manual deployment script execution.
The server-side deployment reaches a successful published state in at least staging, and prod if the configured CI/CD policy supports immediate promotion for this request.
Server-side health/smoke checks pass after deployment.
Any failures are diagnosed, fixed or retried through the controller, and recorded with evidence.
P000.title: Deploy reasoning streaming through Release Controller CI/CD

P001.description: Before deploying, the exact Release Controller API/config, branch policy, namespaces, health URLs, and current service status must be known so the release is triggered correctly and verified against the right endpoints.
success_criteria: Identify the controller config file, HTTP endpoint/base URL, branch trigger policy, namespace target, and health/smoke URLs.
Confirm the controller is reachable and report current status/runs.
Confirm direct deploy scripts are controller internals rather than the deployment path.
Produce exact commands/URLs to use for the release trigger.
P001.title: Discover Release Controller deployment contract and current status

P002.description: The implemented changes are local and span subrepos plus root ledger/submodule pointers. CI/CD cannot publish the change until the relevant commits are created and pushed to the canonical remote branch.
success_criteria: Touched subrepos have focused tests passing immediately before commit or already cited from the current construction.
`novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`, and `novaic-app` changes are committed and pushed.
Root repo records updated submodule pointers and deployment ledger state in a commit and pushes to the canonical branch.
Unrelated files such as `CLAUDE.md` are not accidentally included.
P002.title: Commit and push deployable source state

P003.description: After push, Release Controller must build immutable images and deploy server-side services through its control plane. The run must be observed until terminal success or a concrete failure is diagnosed and retried through the controller.
success_criteria: Trigger uses Release Controller API/control plane for the pushed commit/branch.
Run ID and commit SHA are recorded.
Run reaches a terminal successful state for server-side deployment, or a failure is repaired/retried through controller.
Build/deploy/smoke plan execution result is persisted in the run record.
P003.title: Trigger Release Controller server deployment and observe run completion

P004.description: A controller success is not enough without confirming the server endpoints are live after deployment. The deployment needs a final health/smoke pass and a concise evidence package.
success_criteria: API/backend server health or smoke endpoints pass for the deployed namespace.
Registry/service state is consistent enough to prove server-side services came up.
Final evidence includes commit SHAs, controller run ID, namespace, health URLs, and residual risks.
Ledger validates/renders complete after closure.
P004.title: Verify published server health and close deployment evidence

P005.description: The deployment must use the controller correctly, so local docs/config/source must reveal the expected trigger API, namespaces, deploy scripts, health checks, and branch policy.
success_criteria: Identify config file paths and important values for controller URL/port, branch, namespace, health URLs, and deploy script commands.
Identify whether prod promotion is automatic or manual/promotion API only.
Identify any required dry_run settings or guardrails that affect triggering.
P005.title: Inspect local Release Controller contract

P006.description: Local config is not enough; the controller must be reachable and its current status/runs must be known before triggering a deployment.
success_criteria: Discover a reachable controller base URL from local config, process list, compose, nginx, or docs.
Call status/runs/read endpoints successfully or record the exact blocker.
Capture current latest run/pointers before triggering new release.
P006.title: Probe live Release Controller status and runs

P007.description: Before committing deployable source, touched code should have fresh focused verification evidence so the pushed commit is not stale relative to local tests.
success_criteria: Focused tests pass for `novaic-llm-factory` streaming route/provider behavior.
Focused tests pass for `novaic-agent-runtime` stream parsing, handler, and projection behavior.
Focused tests pass for `novaic-app` timeline/contract guard behavior or App is explicitly marked non-server-release-only.
Git status for touched repos is captured before commit.
P007.title: Run final pre-push focused verification

P008.description: Submodule repositories must be committed and pushed before the root repository can point at their deployable SHAs.
success_criteria: `novaic-llm-factory` commit exists on `origin/main`.
`novaic-agent-runtime` commit exists on `origin/main`.
`novaic-app` commit exists on its remote branch if source changes are retained.
Commit SHAs and push results are recorded.
P008.title: Commit and push touched subrepos

P009.description: The Release Controller reads the root repo commit, so root must record updated submodule pointers for server-relevant repos and the deployment ledger state.
success_criteria: Root commit includes updated submodule pointers for pushed subrepo commits and `.complex-problems` deployment/reasoning ledgers.
Unrelated `CLAUDE.md` is not staged.
Root `origin/main` contains the commit to trigger.
Final root commit SHA is recorded for Release Controller trigger.
P009.title: Commit and push root submodule pointers and deployment ledger

done: Identified controller local API: `http://127.0.0.1:19880` on the API host.
Identified public read-only dashboard: `https://api.gradievo.com/cicd/dashboard`; write APIs are not public through Nginx.
Identified staging trigger: `POST /v1/triggers` body `{"branch":"main","commit":"<sha>"}`; omit `dry_run` for real release.
Identified prod promotion: `POST /v1/promotions/prod` with immutable `api_image`, `factory_image`, `commit`, and optional `promoted_from`.
Identified branch rules in `novaic-release-controller/config.sample.json`: `main -> auto_deploy staging`, `preview/* -> auto_deploy preview-{slug}`, `release/* -> candidate_only`.

Identified deploy commands planned by controller: build/push API and Factory images, then `deploy services-image <namespace> <api-image>` and `deploy factory-image <namespace> <factory-image>` with Release Controller env identity.
Identified health URLs in sample config: staging `https://staging-api.gradievo.com/api/health`, prod `https://api.gradievo.com/health`.
known_gaps: This was local contract inspection only; live controller reachability and actual configured state are handled by P006.
R000.summary: Read local release-controller architecture, deploy runbook, source, and sample config. The deployment contract is explicit: branch releases use `/v1/triggers` with `branch` and `commit`, `main` maps to `staging`, prod is promotion-only, and `deploy services-image` / `deploy factory-image` are controller internal executor steps.

artifacts: Live base for write via API host: `http://127.0.0.1:19880` from `root@api.gradievo.com`.
done: Public read-only status/rules/runs endpoints work at `https://api.gradievo.com/cicd/v1/...`.
Host-local health works through SSH: `curl http://127.0.0.1:19880/health` returned `{"status":"healthy"}`.
Live controller container is healthy: `novaic-release-controller-release_controller-1`, image `127.0.0.1:5000/novaic/release-controller`, port `127.0.0.1:19880->19880`.
Live config confirms repo path `/opt/novaic/release-controller/worktree`, registry `127.0.0.1:5000/novaic/{api-backend,llm-factory}`, `main -> staging`, and `polling_enabled=false`.
Current releases before deployment:
prod: commit `561195e3bf429152879c6100e585adbe0a7b9577`, run `20260525-094505-promote-prod-staging-561195e3bf42`, promoted from staging.
known_gaps: No write trigger was sent in this discovery leaf. Later deployment must use SSH/host-local `POST /v1/triggers` for write API access.
R001.summary: The live Release Controller is reachable and healthy. Public read-only `/cicd` APIs work, and host-local API is available through SSH on the API host. Current staging and prod both point to commit `561195e3bf429152879c6100e585adbe0a7b9577` before this deployment.
verification: `ssh root@api.gradievo.com 'cat /opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json'` was summarized for non-secret config.
`ssh root@api.gradievo.com 'docker ps --filter name=novaic-release-controller ...'` showed the controller container healthy.

done: P005/R000/C000: local contract inspected from docs, config sample, and source.
P006/R001/C001: live controller status, rules, config, container health, and current release pointers captured.
Exact write trigger path identified: SSH to API host and `POST http://127.0.0.1:19880/v1/triggers` with `branch=main`, `commit=<pushed-root-sha>`.
known_gaps: No code was committed or deployed in this discovery phase.
R002.summary: Completed discovery for the controller deployment contract and live state. Release Controller is the correct path; `main` releases to `staging` through `/v1/triggers`, prod is promotion-only, and the live controller is healthy with staging/prod currently at commit `561195e3bf429152879c6100e585adbe0a7b9577`.
verification: Public read-only status/rules/runs endpoints are reachable at `https://api.gradievo.com/cicd/v1/...`.

artifacts: Test output summaries and git status snapshots.
done: Ran Factory focused streaming tests.
Ran Runtime focused streaming/projection tests.
Ran App focused timeline/contract tests.
Captured git status for root and touched subrepos before committing.
known_gaps: Root `CLAUDE.md` is untracked and unrelated; it must not be staged in later commits.
R003.summary: All focused pre-push tests passed across Factory, Runtime, and App. Dirty status is known and limited to intended construction files plus unrelated root `CLAUDE.md`.
verification: `cd novaic-app && npm run test:unit -- src/data/entities/entangledEntityContracts.test.ts src/components/Visual/ActivityTimeline.guard.test.ts src/components/Visual/ActivityTimeline.test.tsx src/components/hooks/useActivityTimeline.test.ts` -> 4 files, 29 tests passed.
Git status shows intended dirty files in `novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`, `novaic-app`, and root `.complex-problems` plus submodule pointer changes.

done: `novaic-llm-factory`: committed and pushed `d846b1d9bba721aa126af456cb9f7518398a8779` (`Add streaming chat completion support`).
`novaic-agent-runtime`: committed and pushed `d9a7b3d8dc85f72d25aae0d176e20035f51315c6` (`Stream reasoning through factory client`).
`novaic-app`: committed and pushed `5cbfdf719cea93b8568f83aa235fd3a7caea1ee5` (`Render streaming reasoning activity updates`).
known_gaps: Root repo has not yet committed the updated submodule pointers or ledger state; that is P009.
R004.summary: Committed and pushed all touched subrepos to their `origin/main` branches. Server-relevant subrepos now have pushed SHAs available for the root submodule pointer update.
verification: Each subrepo push completed successfully to its GitHub remote.
Each touched subrepo reports `## main...origin/main` with no dirty files after push.
Root now sees modified submodule pointers for `novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`, and `novaic-app`.

acceptance_criteria: The controller trigger mechanism, namespaces, branch policy, health URLs, and current runtime status are known before triggering release.
Touched code is committed and pushed in subrepos, then root submodule pointers and ledger are committed/pushed.
Release Controller receives an explicit trigger for the pushed commit/branch.
Controller run reaches success for server-side services, with run record/plan execution evidence.
Public or internal health/smoke endpoints confirm the deployed server is serving after release.
Failures are retried or repaired through the same controller path, not via direct manual deployment scripts.
assumptions: `main` is the canonical branch for release-controller CI/CD unless the controller config says otherwise.
Server-side means API backend/Runtime/Factory stack; App desktop release is not required for the minimum success condition.
T000.classification_reason: Deployment is a broad multi-phase CI/CD operation with independent discovery, commit/push, controller trigger, and live verification phases.
T000.problem_definition: Local reasoning streaming changes exist across subrepos and must be shipped through the centralized Release Controller CI/CD flow. The deployment must not bypass the controller by directly running manual deploy scripts.
T000.proposed_solution: Split deployment into sequential, verifiable phases: discover the exact Release Controller configuration/API; prepare and push commits/submodule pointers; trigger the controller release; observe build/deploy/smoke status; and close with live service health verification and cleanup of any deployment-specific residue.
risks: Controller may reject prod automation by policy; if so, finish staging server publication and use promotion only if policy allows or the request explicitly authorizes it.
Remote host credentials or controller URL may live in local config; discover from existing repo/deploy docs rather than guessing.
Unrelated untracked files should not be swept into deployment commits.
T000.title: Release Controller deployment execution plan
T000.verification_plan: Use Release Controller docs/config/API plus git status, focused tests, controller run records, and HTTP health checks. Record exact run IDs, commit SHAs, URLs, and command outcomes.

acceptance_criteria: Local docs/config identify the intended trigger endpoint, branch/namespace policy, deploy steps, and health URLs.
Live controller status/runs are reachable or the blocker is explicit.
The final discovery result gives exact trigger parameters for later deployment.
assumptions: Release Controller code/config under `novaic-release-controller`, `docker/release-controller`, and `docs/*` is authoritative unless live status contradicts it.
T001.classification_reason: Per the user's split-before-third-level rule, P001 is a second-level discovery phase and should split into local contract inspection and live controller probing.
T001.problem_definition: Release must be driven through the centralized Release Controller. We need exact local configuration and live controller status before changing remote state.
T001.proposed_solution: Split discovery into local contract inspection and live controller probing. Local inspection should read docs/config/scripts to identify endpoint, trigger API, branch rules, namespaces, and health checks. Live probing should call the controller read APIs to confirm reachability and current runs.
risks: Config may use host-only URLs or remote paths; avoid guessing and use the checked-in controller config or service unit where possible.
T001.title: Discover controller contract through docs/config and live status
T001.verification_plan: Use `sed`, `rg`, and HTTP calls against configured controller endpoints. Record file paths and API responses in child results.

acceptance_criteria: Local controller contract is summarized with file evidence.
Trigger and promotion API shapes are identified.
Namespace/branch/health/deploy-script values are identified or marked absent.
assumptions: Checked-in docs/source are trustworthy for controller behavior.
T002.classification_reason: P005 is a third-level leaf with bounded read-only local contract inspection and direct evidence.
T002.problem_definition: Deployment trigger parameters need to be derived from checked-in docs/config/source.
T002.proposed_solution: Read Release Controller docs, compose/config files, service source, planner, and deployment runbooks to extract the trigger API, namespace policy, deploy commands, health URLs, dry-run behavior, and prod promotion policy.
risks: Some secrets/host URLs may be configured only on the server, so local discovery may need the live probe child for final endpoint confirmation.
T002.title: Read local Release Controller docs and config
T002.verification_plan: Use source reads and `rg` against the release-controller and deploy docs.

acceptance_criteria: Live status, rules, and recent runs are captured from the actual controller.
Current staging/prod pointers are known before triggering.
Any reachability issue is explicit with command output.
assumptions: `root@api.gradievo.com` SSH is configured if public read-only routes are insufficient.
T003.classification_reason: P006 is a third-level leaf with bounded live read-only probing and clear evidence.
T003.problem_definition: The deployment needs the live controller base URL/status/runs before triggering release state changes.
T003.proposed_solution: Try public read-only `/cicd` APIs first, then SSH/API-host local curl if needed. Capture status, rules, recent runs, current releases, and controller health.
risks: Public read-only route may not expose `/health`; use the host-local API through SSH for write-trigger confirmation.
T003.title: Probe live controller read APIs
T003.verification_plan: Use `curl -fsS` and, if needed, `ssh root@api.gradievo.com 'curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:19880/...'`.

acceptance_criteria: Final focused tests or already-current test evidence is available before commit.
Server-relevant subrepos (`novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`) are committed and pushed.
App source changes are committed and pushed as source hygiene, even though server release only consumes backend/factory/runtime submodules.
Root repo records updated submodule pointers plus deployment/reasoning-streaming ledgers and pushes to `origin/main`.
`CLAUDE.md` remains untracked and not included.
assumptions: Current branch `main` is the intended branch in root and touched subrepos.
T004.classification_reason: P002 is a second-level commit/push phase with independent verification, subrepo commit, and root pointer commit slices.
T004.problem_definition: The release-controller can only deploy pushed commits. The local implementation spans subrepos and root ledger/submodule state, so commits must be created in the right order and pushed cleanly.
T004.proposed_solution: Split into final pre-push verification/status capture, subrepo commits/pushes, and root submodule/ledger commit/push. Exclude unrelated untracked files.
risks: Subrepo push ordering matters; root pointer must point at pushed subrepo commits.
Root may have unrelated dirty files; stage explicitly.
T004.title: Commit and push source for Release Controller deployment
T004.verification_plan: Use git status/log/push output and focused test commands. Verify root commit SHA after push.

acceptance_criteria: Factory focused tests pass.
Runtime focused tests pass.
App focused tests pass.
Dirty file list is known for explicit staging.
assumptions: These focused tests are sufficient for local pre-push; Release Controller quality gates are authoritative CI after push.
T005.classification_reason: P007 is a third-level verification leaf with fixed test commands and explicit pass/fail evidence.
T005.problem_definition: The commit/push stage needs current test evidence for the touched paths.
T005.proposed_solution: Run focused tests in Factory, Runtime, and App, plus capture git status summaries before commits.
risks: A failure here blocks committing until fixed.
T005.title: Run focused pre-push checks
T005.verification_plan: Run the exact focused test commands and `git status --short` in touched repos and root.

acceptance_criteria: Factory commit pushed to `origin/main`.
Runtime commit pushed to `origin/main`.
App commit pushed to `origin/main`.
No unrelated files are staged.
assumptions: All touched subrepos are on `main` tracking `origin/main`.
T006.classification_reason: P008 is a third-level execution leaf with explicit files to stage, recent tests, and direct push verification.
T006.problem_definition: Subrepo commits must exist on remotes before root records submodule pointers for CI/CD.
T006.proposed_solution: Explicitly stage construction-owned files in `novaic-llm-factory`, `novaic-agent-runtime`, and `novaic-app`; commit with concise messages; push each `main` branch; record SHAs.
risks: Remote push can fail if branch moved; use non-destructive pull/rebase only if needed and inspect first.
T006.title: Commit and push touched submodule repositories
T006.verification_plan: Use `git status`, `git diff --cached`, `git commit`, `git push`, and `git rev-parse HEAD` in each subrepo.

acceptance_criteria: Root commit points `novaic-llm-factory` to `d846b1d9bba721aa126af456cb9f7518398a8779`.
Root commit points `novaic-agent-runtime` to `d9a7b3d8dc85f72d25aae0d176e20035f51315c6`.
Root commit records the App pointer and complex-problems ledgers/index.
`CLAUDE.md` is not staged.
Root commit is pushed to `origin/main`, and SHA is recorded.
assumptions: Committing ledger state before final deployment closure is acceptable because the release images do not copy root `.complex-problems` into the API backend or Factory images.
T007.classification_reason: P009 is a third-level bounded git operation with explicit files to stage and a direct push verification.
T007.problem_definition: The root repository must point to the pushed subrepo commits before Release Controller can build/deploy them from a root commit.
T007.proposed_solution: Stage only updated submodule pointers and `.complex-problems` ledger/index artifacts, exclude unrelated `CLAUDE.md`, commit to `main`, push to `origin/main`, and record the root commit SHA for the trigger.
risks: Additional ledger changes after this commit may remain local; the release trigger should use the pushed root SHA from this step.
T007.title: Commit root release pointer state
T007.verification_plan: Use explicit `git add`, `git diff --cached --stat`, `git diff --cached --submodule=log`, `git status`, `git commit`, `git push`, and `git rev-parse HEAD`.
